events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need identify component/condition: flat/horizontal vent below flood rim. In plumbing, vents must be installed with grade/slope to drain back to drainage system, not flat/horizontal, and must terminate above flood rim of fixtures served. A flat/horizontal vent below flood rim is non-compliant. Condition installed-defective, non-compliant. Decision fail. Risk high? Could allow sewer gas/health hazard, maybe high. Work stage rough-complete. Percent complete maybe 80? Value 0. Sound etc default. Findings: vent installed level/horizontal, below flood rim; lacks required slope to drain; improper vent termination height. Actions: reconfigure vent to slope back to drain at min 1/4" per foot, raise above flood rim, proper support. References: IPC 906.2/UPC 906.1 vent termination, IPC 905 slope of vent? Need be accurate. Let's recall IPC: Section 905 Vent connection and grade: vents shall be graded to drain back to soil or waste pipe by gravity, at least 1/4 inch per foot or sloped to drain. Section 906 Vent termination: must terminate outside not less than 6 inches above roof or flood rim of highest fixture? Actually vent terminals through roof min 6" above roof. For air admittance etc. Flood rim: fixture vent connections? IPC 906.2? UPC 906.1. Also horizontal branch vents? The scenario says flat/horizontal vent below flood rim. Could be a "dry vent" taken off horizontally below flood rim of fixture, which is prohibited: dry vent connections for fixture traps must be located downstream of trap and above flood rim? Actually IPC 906.1: Each vent shall terminate outside as required, but connection: vent connecting at fixture? Let's think.

The defect cues: vent pipe run horizontally without slope; installed below flood rim of fixture; may act as waste line rather than vent; potential for water/solid accumulation, blockage, sewer gas; violates vent grade and termination height.

Need produce JSON with fields. Ensure valid JSON. No prose outside.

events.label: response

  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",

  "percent_complete": 75,
  "value": 0,

  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Vent pipe is installed flat/horizontal with no drainage slope",
    "Vent is located below the flood rim of the served fixture",
    "Configuration can allow waste/solids to enter the vent and block it",
    "Sewer gas may not be properly vented, creating health and odor hazards"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove and reconfigure the vent with proper grade back to the drainage system",
    "Ensure the vent terminates above the flood rim of the highest fixture served",
    "Confirm vent sizing, support, and cleanout access per code",
    "Re-inspect before closing walls"
  ],
  "rationale": "A vent must slope to drain back to the waste system and must terminate above fixture flood rims to function as a vent and prevent sewer gas entry. A flat, horizontal vent below the flood rim is non-compliant and can trap solids or allow waste flow into the vent.",
  "references": [
    "IPC 905.1 / UPC 905.1 - vent grade and drainage back to soil/waste pipe",
    "IPC 906.2 / UPC 906.1 - vent termination above flood rim and required height"
  ]
}
